summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several recent developments in small UAS activity. A militia group, Kataib Hezbollah, released video showing an FPV drone strike on Victory Base near Baghdad airport, which experts say bypassed U.S. defenses. In Ukraine, Russian FPV drones struck multiple civilian targets, killing a woman in Kozacha Lopan, two medics in the Kharkiv region, and wounding two civilians in Rozumivka and two men in a Slatyne store. Ukrainian engineers developed the "Chipa" net gun to physically snare FPV drones when other air defenses fail, while residents in the Kharkiv region cut fiber-optic cables to disrupt Ukrainian FPV drone operations. Russia is expanding its drone force to 101,000 troops by April and has shifted from mass drone production to missile use, while Ukraine is accelerating FPV drone development and revamping military training to focus on FPV drone defense.
